values.value:
== NAMED CREDENTIAL XML FORMAT == File path: namedCredentials/ApiName.namedCredential metadataType: NamedCredential memberName: ApiName (no __c suffix)
IDENTITY TYPE is always Named Principal → principalType = NamedUser Pick the correct protocol template based on what the ticket specifies.
CRITICAL — ELEMENT ORDER: elements MUST appear in strict alphabetical order. Salesforce rejects Named Credential XML with wrong element order. Do NOT include <fullName> inside the XML body — the file name is the identity.
-- PROTOCOL: No Authentication -- Use when ticket says: "No Authentication", "No Auth", "anonymous" <?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?> <NamedCredential xmlns="http://soap.sforce.com/2006/04/metadata"> <allowMergeFieldsInBody>false</allowMergeFieldsInBody> <allowMergeFieldsInHeader>false</allowMergeFieldsInHeader> <calloutStatus>Enabled</calloutStatus> <endpoint>https://api.example.com</endpoint> <generateAuthorizationHeader>true</generateAuthorizationHeader> <label>Label</label> <principalType>NamedUser</principalType> <protocol>NoAuthentication</protocol> </NamedCredential>
-- PROTOCOL: OAuth 2.0 -- Use when ticket says: "OAuth 2.0", "OAuth" Required from ticket: Authentication Provider name + Scope <?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?> <NamedCredential xmlns="http://soap.sforce.com/2006/04/metadata"> <allowMergeFieldsInBody>false</allowMergeFieldsInBody> <allowMergeFieldsInHeader>false</allowMergeFieldsInHeader> <authProvider>AuthProviderDeveloperName</authProvider> <calloutStatus>Enabled</calloutStatus> <endpoint>https://api.example.com</endpoint> <generateAuthorizationHeader>true</generateAuthorizationHeader> <label>Label</label> <oauthScope>api refresh_token</oauthScope> <principalType>NamedUser</principalType> <protocol>Oauth</protocol> </NamedCredential>
-- PROTOCOL: Password Authentication -- Use when ticket says: "Password Authentication", "Password Auth", "Basic Auth" Required from ticket: Username + Password <?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?> <NamedCredential xmlns="http://soap.sforce.com/2006/04/metadata"> <allowMergeFieldsInBody>false</allowMergeFieldsInBody> <allowMergeFieldsInHeader>false</allowMergeFieldsInHeader> <calloutStatus>Enabled</calloutStatus> <endpoint>https://api.example.com</endpoint> <generateAuthorizationHeader>true</generateAuthorizationHeader> <label>Label</label> <password>thepassword</password> <principalType>NamedUser</principalType> <protocol>Password</protocol> <username>user@example.com</username> </NamedCredential>
RULES: - principalType is ALWAYS NamedUser — never Anonymous - calloutStatus is ALWAYS Enabled - generateAuthorizationHeader is ALWAYS true - allowMergeFieldsInBody and allowMergeFieldsInHeader default to false unless ticket says otherwise - Element order MUST be strictly alphabetical — any deviation causes deploy failure - Do NOT include <fullName> inside the XML body - For OAuth 2.0: authProvider value = the DeveloperName of the Auth Provider stated in the ticket - For OAuth 2.0: oauthScope value = scope string stated in the ticket (e.g. "api refresh_token") - For Password: username and password come directly from the ticket - NEVER invent or guess authProvider, username, or password — use only what the ticket provides - Each Named Credential is its own separate file — do NOT merge into one file
-- UPDATE -- The current state of all Named Credentials is in orgState.existingNamedCredentials. Each entry contains: apiName, label, endpoint, protocol, principalType, calloutStatus, generateAuthorizationHeader, allowMergeFieldsInBody, allowMergeFieldsInHeader, and protocol-specific fields: authProvider + oauthScope (OAuth), username (Password).
To UPDATE a Named Credential: 1. Find the NC in existingNamedCredentials by apiName 2. If NOT found in orgState → return an error in summary, do NOT generate any files 3. Regenerate the COMPLETE XML using ALL existing values from orgState 4. Only change the specific field(s) the ticket requests 5. Preserve protocol — do NOT change protocol unless ticket explicitly requests it 6. For Password protocol: username comes from orgState (already known). password is NEVER returned by any API — it MUST come from the ticket. If the ticket does not provide a password → return error in summary, do NOT deploy 7. Use metadataType=NamedCredential, memberName=apiName, filePath=namedCredentials/{apiName}.namedCredential
-- DELETE -- To DELETE a Named Credential: 1. Confirm the apiName exists in existingNamedCredentials in orgState 2. If NOT found → return error in summary, do NOT generate destructiveFiles 3. Use the shorthand destructiveFiles entry (no xmlContent needed — Apex builds it): { "metadataType": "NamedCredential", "member": "My_API" } 4. Do NOT generate any files[] entry for delete 5. Do NOT generate xmlContent — Apex builds destructiveChangesPost.xml automatically
Named Credential XML format for No Auth, OAuth2, Password protocols
